name: python-env
description: Fast Python environment management with uv (10-100x faster than pip). Triggers on: uv, venv, pip, pyproject, python environment, install package, dependencies.
capabilities: Create virtual environments with uv venv
Generate lock files with uv pip compile
author: 0xDarkMatter
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown reference files for Python environment management using uv. No executable code, no network calls, no file system access beyond documentation files. All 176 static findings are false positives - the scanner misidentifies markdown code block examples as executable shell commands and documentation URLs as network indicators. This is a reference skill providing guidance on uv CLI usage patterns.
valueStatement: Creating and managing Python virtual environments requires learning multiple tools and flags. This skill provides instant access to uv patterns for fast environment setup, package installation, and dependency management.
limitations: Requires uv CLI tool to be installed separately
Reference documentation only - no automatic execution

useCases.title: Set up new Python projects
useCases.description: Quickly create venv and install dependencies for new Python projects

useCases.title: Manage data science environments
useCases.description: Create isolated environments for data science work with reproducible dependencies

useCases.title: Automate dependency workflows
useCases.description: Generate lock files and configure CI/CD pipelines for Python projects

promptTemplates.title: Create venv
promptTemplates.scenario: Need a new Python virtual environment
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Python virtual environment using uv with Python 3.11

promptTemplates.title: Install packages
promptTemplates.prompt: Install requests and flask using uv pip with version constraints

promptTemplates.prompt: Set up a uv workspace with multiple Python packages

bestPractices: Always use lock files for reproducible production deployments
Separate development and production dependencies using optional-dependencies
Pin Python version in pyproject.toml to avoid compatibility issues
antiPatterns: Using pip instead of uv for slower installs
Committing generated pycache or venv directories
Using loose version constraints without lock files in production

faq.question: Is uv faster than pip?
faq.answer: Yes, uv is 10-100x faster than pip for package installation.

faq.question: Can I use uv with existing pip requirements.txt files?
faq.answer: Yes, uv pip install -r requirements.txt works directly with pip requirements files.

faq.question: Does uv work on Windows?
faq.answer: Yes, uv supports Windows, macOS, and Linux operating systems.

faq.question: Are uv environments compatible with VSCode?
faq.answer: Yes, point VSCode Python interpreter to .venv/Scripts/python.exe on Windows.

faq.question: How do I update packages with uv?
faq.answer: Use uv pip compile with --upgrade flag to update all packages to latest versions.
